evals.name: create-column-and-cards
evals.prompt: On my proj-kanban board, create a new column called 'Q3 Launch' with a teal color (#14b8a6). Then add two status cards to it: one titled 'Design review' with status pending, and one titled 'API contract' (leave it on the default status).
evals.expected_output: Agent calls POST /projects with name 'Q3 Launch' and color #14b8a6, then POST /projects/:id/cards twice (resolving the new project id from the create response or GET /projects). 'Design review' has status=pending; 'API contract' uses default status (active). Final board state contains the column with both cards.

evals.name: update-card-status-and-memo
evals.prompt: The card titled 'Design review' on my kanban board is finished. Mark it as done and set its memo to 'approved by team'.
evals.expected_output: Agent first GETs /projects to find the card id by title, then PUT /cards/:id with status=done and memo='approved by team' (partial update, not clobbering title). Other fields unchanged.

evals.name: read-board-find-blocked
evals.prompt: Give me a summary of my kanban board: list each column and tell me which columns currently have any blocked cards.
evals.expected_output: Agent makes a single GET /projects call and parses the nested cards arrays, reporting per-column which have at least one card with status=blocked. No mutations.

evals.name: move-card-create-target
evals.prompt: Move the 'API contract' card into a column called 'Backlog'. If there's no 'Backlog' column yet, create it first.
evals.expected_output: Agent GETs /projects to resolve ids, creates 'Backlog' via POST /projects if missing, then PUT /cards/:id with project_id set to the Backlog project id to move the card. Card keeps its title/status.

evals.name: delete-column-cascade
evals.prompt: Delete the entire 'Q3 Launch' column from my kanban board, including all of its cards.
evals.expected_output: Agent GETs /projects to resolve the 'Q3 Launch' id, then DELETE /projects/:id. Recognizes that the API cascade-deletes the column's cards automatically (no need to delete cards individually) and that this is irreversible.

evals.name: base-url-path
evals.prompt: I need to call this kanban's REST API from a separate script. The server listens on port 10023 with default settings. Give me the exact full URL and HTTP method to (a) list all projects and (b) create a new project.
evals.expected_output: Uses the full base path http://localhost:10023/proj-kanban/api/projects (GET to list, POST to create), not /api/projects or /projects.

evals.name: no-reorder-endpoint
evals.prompt: Reorder the board columns so that 'Backend' becomes the first (leftmost) column.
evals.expected_output: Explains the API has no reorder capability: position is insert-only and never updatable; visual order lives in browser localStorage. Does not invent an endpoint or claim success.

evals.name: created-status-code-200
evals.prompt: I'm writing an automated test that creates a project via the API and asserts on the HTTP response. What status code does a successful POST /projects return, and what is in the response body?
evals.expected_output: States success is HTTP 200 (not 201) and the body is the full created project object.

evals.name: put-empty-string-footgun
evals.prompt: Change ONLY the color of the 'Backend' column to #ef4444. Be careful not to accidentally change anything else about it.
evals.expected_output: PUT /projects/:id with only {color}; omits name (does not send name:'' which would wipe it); aware that '' overwrites on PUT.

evals.name: no-per-item-get
evals.prompt: Fetch the details (title, status, memo) of just the card with id 10.
evals.expected_output: No per-item GET exists; uses GET /projects and filters the nested cards for id 10.

evals.name: move-no-validation-orphan
evals.prompt: Move the card with id 11 into the column whose id is 99.
evals.expected_output: Knows PUT /cards/:id with project_id moves a card but is NOT validated; moving to a non-existent id 99 returns 200 yet orphans the card. Verifies id 99 exists first; flags that it doesn't.
